original: Roman EmperorTheodosius Iproclaims his six year old son Arcadius co-emperor
Benedict VI is elected as the Catholic Pope
Supplies to build Portuguese trading post Castelo de São Jorge da Mina (Elmina Castle) arrive on the Gold Coast (now Ghana), first European building south of the Sahara
France cedes Roussillon & Cerdagne to Spain by treaty of Barcelona
Italian city Mirandola surrenders to the French
Henry Howard, Earl of Surrey, is executed in the Tower of London for treason
San Agustin Church in Manila is officially completed; the oldest church in the Philippines
Battle of Golden Hill (Lower Manhattan)
First manned balloon flight in Ireland
Democratic revolution in Amsterdam results in establishment of shot-lived Batavian Republic
United Kingdom reoccupies the Cape of Good Hope following victory in the Battle of Blaauwberg over French vassal, the Batavian Republic, establishing British rule in South Africa
Louis Napoleon signs 1st Dutch aviation law
Cold Friday: temperature at Portsmouth, New Hampshire drops from 54°F to minus 12°F in one day with many frozen to death
Ezra Daggett and nephew Thomas Kensett patent food storage in tin cans
Aden conquered by British East India Company
Mexican and indigenous Pueblo warriors kill territorial governor Charles Bent (47), and 5 others in a revolt against new American rulers in Taos, New Mexico Territory
Georgia secedes from the Union (US Civil War)
Mississippi troops take Fort Massachusetts and Ship Island (US Civil War)
Battle of Mill Springs, Kentucky (Fishing Creek, Logan's Crossroads)
General Mieroslawski appointed dictator of Poland
NV Suriname Bank established
Union occupies Fort Anderson, North Carolina
1st Negro lodge of US Masons approved, New Jersey
Battle of Abu Kru (Battle of Gubat), Sudan: British Desert Column defeat Mahdist forces: 121 British and untold Mahdists killed
Aurora Ski Club, 1st in US, founded in Minnesota
Henrik Ibsen's play "The Master Builder" premieres in Berlin
Brown defeats Harvard 6-0 in 1st intercollegiate hockey game
Anglo-Egyptian Sudan forms
1st regular transatlantic radio broadcast between US & England
Eugene Walter's "Easiest Way" premieres in NYC
Germany & Bolivia end commerce and friendship treaty
US National Institute of Arts & Letters incorporated by Congress
Neon Tube sign patented by George Claude
The Silvertown explosion: 73 die when a munitions factory in Essex explodes.
Finnish Civil War: The first serious battles between the Red Guards and the White Guard
Soviets disallow a Constitution Assembly
National elections held in Germany to form a National Constituent Assembly and draft a constitution
Costa Rica, Guatemala, Honduras & El Salvador sign Pact of Union
Geological survey says US oil supply will be depleted in 20 years
WMC-AM in Memphis TN begins radio transmissions
-48°F (-44°C), Van Buren, Maine (state record)
British government decides to send troops to China
Acadia National Park, Maine established
Charlie Conacher becomes first Toronto player to score 5 goals in an NHL game as Maple Leafs rout the NY Americans, 11-3 at Maple Leaf Gardens
Coopers Inc. sells the world's first men's briefs in Chicago, calls it the "Jockey"
KLM begins flight path between Curacao & Aruba
General Motors begins mass production of diesel engines
Ernest Hausen of Wisconsin sets chicken-plucking record of 4.4 sec
LPGA Titleholders Championship Women's Golf, Augusta CC: Helen Hicks wins by 1 stroke ahead of Helen Dettweiler
British offensive in Eritrea
British troops occupies Kassalaf, Sudan
Dutch Catholic priest and outspoken anti-Nazi Titus Brandsma arrested by German occupiers - later dies at Dachau concentration camp; posthumously canonized by Pope Francis, in 2022
Japanese forces invade Burma
Joint Chiefs of Staff decide on invasion in Sicily
SS Himera runs aground at Athens, kills 392
Cuba recognises Israel.
Maiden flight by Canada's Avro Canada CF-100 military plane
NFL purchases struggling football franchise, New York Yanks from owner Ted Collins; moves club to Dallas, Texas
PGA approves allowing black participants
"The Millionaire" TV series premieres on CBS
USSR performs atmospheric nuclear test
Canadian Football Council renamed Canadian Football League
PresidentEisenhowerand Prime Minister Nobusuke Kishi sign the US-Japan Security Treaty
KFME TV channel 13 in Fargo, North Dakota (PBS) begins broadcasting
British composer Michael Tippett's cantata "Vision of St Augustine" premieres in London
Herr Karl Tausch writes shortest will "Vse Zene" (All to wife)
WKBF TV channel 61 in Cleveland, Ohio (IND) begins broadcasting
Eight Bishops of the Roman Catholic Church in the Netherlands say there should be room in the church for both married priests and celibate priests
NHL Writers' Association renamed Professional Hockey Writers' Association
Belgium government of Leburton falls
China and South Vietnam clash over the Paracel Islands, resulting in a Chinese victory
Notre Dame beats UCLA, 71-70 in South Bend, Indiana to end Bruins' NCAA-record 88-game basketball win streak
4 mail truck assault on El Al B-747 in Paris, escape to Iraq
Triple J begins broadcasting in Sydney, Australia.
Snow falls in Miami, Florida. This is the only time in the history of the city that snowfall has occurred. It also fell in the Bahamas.
US President Ford pardons Iva Toguri D'Aquino (Tokyo Rose)
World's largest crowd gathering - the Hindu Kumbh Mela in India attracts a record 15 million people
Eddie Mathews elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
Harlem drug lord Nicky Barnes is sentenced to life in prison and a $125,000 fine after being convicted on narcotics racketeering charges
John N Mitchell (former US Attorney General) released on parole from federal prison
US & Iran sign agreement to release 52 American hostages
Aust-WI one-day game that produced a Privy Council libel case
Heater explodes at Star Elementary School, Oklahoma, kills 6 children & teacher
California Supreme Court rejects the request of quadriplegic Elizabeth Bouvia's to starve herself to death in a public hospital
4 die in a car & train crash in Buda Ill
Cerebral Palsy telethon raises $17 million
Spain recognizes Israel
Guy Hunt becomes Alabama's 1st Republican governor since 1874
"48 Hours" premieres on CBS-TV
Disabled writer Christopher Nolan wins the Whitbread Book of the Year prize
Police break up protests in Johannesburg against the cricket players defying a boycott on playing in apartheid South Africa
Turning point in the exodus of hundreds of thousands of Kashmiri Hindus from Indian-administrated Kashmir due to violence from Muslim militants
Eastern Airlines shuts down operations
Iraq files SCUD missiles at Tel Aviv and other cities in Israel during the Gulf War
Cerebral Palsy telethon raises $23.5 million
IBM announces a nearly $5B loss for 1992
Israel recognizes PLO as no longer criminal
Oakland As unveil new elephant logo
Robert M Gates, ends term as 15th director of CIA
STS-54 (Endeavour) lands
-20°F (-29°C) (5:32 AM) coldest day ever recorded in Cleveland Ohio
-36°F (-38°C) in New Whiteland, Indiana (state record)
Jean-Claude Juncker (28) sworn in as premier of Luxembourg
NHL Board of Governors approves sale of Winnipeg Jets, officially clearing the way for the team to move to Phoenix, Arizona in time for 1996-97 season

"Tuck Rule Game" AFC Divisional Playoff Game; with under 2 minutes to play New England Patriots trail Oakland Raiders, 13-10 in a driving snowstorm, when a Tom Brady fumble ruled an incomplete pass. Patriots win 16-13 in overtime
A Slovak Air Force Antonov An-24 crashes in Hungary.
Terrorist blows himself up in Tel Aviv, killing only himself but injuring 20 people, one of them seriously
The New Horizons probe is launched by NASA on the first mission to Pluto
Armenian journalist Hrant Dink assassinated in front of his "Agos" newspaper's office by 17-year-old Turkish ultranationalist Ogün Samast
FBI shuts down Megaupload.com for alleged copyright infringement, hacker group Anonymous responds by attacking government and entertainment industry websites
Calcium deposits are discovered on Mars by NASA’s Curiosity Rover
Four climbers are killed by an avalanche in Glen Coe, Scotland
The 2012-2013 NHL season begins after a 119-day lockout
China's economic growth revealed as lowest for 25 years - 6.9% in 2015
High-rise building collapses in Tehran, killing 20 firefighters
The US death toll from COVID-19 passes 400,000
5G cellphone service launches in the US with airlines claiming it could interfere with airplane technologies
Major report on antimicrobial resistance shows 4.95m deaths worldwide associated with drug-resistant bacteria, making untreatable infections now a leading cause of death
Cyclone Cheneso makes landfall in northern Madagascar, killing at least 25 people and leaving about 40,000 homeless
At least 70 miners are killed in a gold mine collapses in Kangaba district, south-west Mali, the latest in a series of small-scale mine disasters
Japan is the fifth nation to land a spacecraft on the Moon with its SLIM (Smart Lander for Investigating Moon) using "pinpoint landing" technology
UK declares national incident over rise of measles cases due to lack of vaccination, WHO’s European region also reports 5-fold increase 2022-2023

Gaza war: Israel-Hamas ceasefire comes into effect in Gaza as the first Israeli hostages are released, with the Palestinian death toll at least 46,800 (lasts until an Israeli surprise attack on 18 March)
TikTok app stops working in the US shortly before a federal ban on the app is due to take effect
Theodosius installed as co-emperor of the Eastern Roman Empire by Emperor Gratian, charged with repelling the Goths
English KingEdward IIIintroduces his Sumptuary Laws, restricting what people ate and wore to preserve social status (largely ignored)
French city of Rouen surrenders toHenry Vin Hundred Years' War
Battle of Bogesund:Christian IIof Denmark defeats the Regent of Sweden, Sten Sture the Younger who is mortally wounded
French KingLouis XIVand Holy Roman Emperor Leopold I sign a secret Partition Treaty, dividing the Spanish Empire if Charles II of Spain died without an heir
Richard Steelepublishes "Crisis" defending Hanoverian success
"Bonnie Prince Charlie", PrinceCharles Edward Stuart's Jacobite army occupies Stirling, Scotland [OS=Jan 8]
Johann Wolfgang von Goethe's tragic play "Faust, Part 1" premieres
Giuseppe Verdi's opera "Il Trovatore" premieres in Rome
The first electric lighting system employing overhead wires, built byThomas Edison, begins service at Roselle, New Jersey
Jules Massenet's opera "Manon" premieres in Paris
Gerhart Hauptmann's play "Und Pippa Tanzt!" premieres in Berlin
Raymond Poincaréinstalled as President of France
UK Chancellor of the ExchequerStanley Baldwinand US Secretary of the TreasuryAndrew Mellonagree to reschedule repayment of Britain's $4.5 billion war debt over 62 years

MLB CommissionerKenesaw Mountain Landisdenies Joe Jackson's appeal for reinstatement into baseball; Jackson was banned after 1919 "Black Sox" World Series
Cy Young,Tris Speakerand Nap Lajorie are elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame
MillionaireHoward Hughessets transcontinental air record (7h 28m 25s)
The Three Stoogesfilm "You Nazty Spy!" about the Nazis released with the disclaimer "Any resemblance between the characters in this picture and any persons, living or dead, is a miracle."
1st Warsaw Ghetto Uprising begins
American track and field olympianJesse Owensnamed Illinois Athletic Commission secretary
1st presidential news conference filmed for TV (Eisenhower)
City of Hoboken, New Jersey dedicates a plaque honoring achievements ofAlexander Cartwrightin organizing early baseball at Elysian Field
1st episode of "TheDick Van DykeShow" is filmed
Indira Gandhiis elected India's first female Prime Minister
Northern Ireland Prime MinisterTerence O'Neillcalls for "a new endeavour by organisations in Northern Ireland to cross denominational barriers and advance the cause of better community relations"

The Beatles' song "Helter Skelter" is played atCharles Mansontrial
Sandy Koufax,Yogi Berra, &Early Wynnelected to Baseball Hall of Fame
Ernie Bankselected to Baseball Hall of Fame
JudgeWilliam H. Websteris appointed Director of the FBI by US President Jimmy Carter

Formula One Constructors Association (FOCA) led byBernie Ecclestone, the Fédération Internationale du Sport Automobile led by Jean Marie Balestre and the Formula One teams agree to the First Concorde Agreement granting FOCA the right to televise Formula One races and requiring all teams to compete in every race under fixed rules
Muhammad Alitalks a despondent 21-year-old out of committing suicide
"Born in the USA", single byBruce Springsteen, peaks at #9
Israeli Prime MinisterShimon Peresvisits Netherlands
US PresidentRonald Reaganpardons NY Yankees ownerGeorge Steinbrennerfor supplying illegal funds for Nixon's re-election campaign
Columbia Records releases "The Wheel", the eighth studio album by American singer-songwriterRosanne Cash. co-produced by Cash and future husband John Leventhal
9th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: The Animals; The Band; Duane Eddy; The Grateful Dead;Elton John;John Lennon;Bob Marley;Rod Stewart; Willie Dixon; and Johnny Otis
Yasser Arafatreturns to Hebron after more than 30 years and joins celebrations over the handover of the last Israeli-controlled West Bank city.
Michael Jordan, who led the Chicago Bulls to 6 NBA championships as a player, returns to the NBA, joining the Washington Wizards as part owner and President of Basketball Operations
Joe Liebermanannounces his retirement from the Senate at the end of his fourth term
Lance Armstrongadmits to doping in all seven of his Tour de France victories
Adama Barrow sworn in as President of Gambia in Dahkar as Senegalese troops enter Gambia to persuade former PresidentYahya Jammehto leave

UK PMBoris Johnsoneases Omicron COVID-19 restrictions, saying the current wave of infections has peaked
New Zealand leaderJacinda Ardernannounces she is stepping down as Prime Minister after five and a half years

After a ten-day siege,Arthur Wellesley, 1stDuke of Wellington, orders British soldiers of the Light and Third Divisions to storm Ciudad Rodrigo during the Peninsular War
World War I: 4 people in Norfolk are killed in the 1st German Zeppelin air raid attack on the United Kingdom
